executive_summary: Penetration testing of scanme.org (45.33.32.156) identified four exposed network services with medium and low risk levels. The primary concerns are SSH (port 22) and HTTP (port 80), both of which require configuration review and patch-level verification. Two additional services on ports 9929 and 31337 are exposed with unknown versions and require validation of business necessity. The target appears to be a legitimate scanning/testing resource; however, standard defensive posture checks should be performed to reduce the attack surface.

findings.title: SSH Service Exposed with Outdated Version (6.6.1p1 Ubuntu 2ubuntu2.13)
findings.evidence: nmap identified SSH service running on port 22 with version 6.6.1p1 Ubuntu 2ubuntu2.13. SSH is externally exposed and serves as a remote administrative access vector.
findings.recommendation: Verify SSH authentication policy by confirming password authentication is disabled and root login is restricted. Review SSH version 6.6.1p1 against current Ubuntu security patches and upgrade if outdated. Implement network segmentation or firewall rules to restrict access to SSH if appropriate.

findings.title: HTTP Web Server Exposed (Apache 2.4.7)
findings.evidence: nmap identified HTTP service running on port 80 with Apache version 2.4.7. The web server is externally exposed and may introduce application-layer and configuration-based risks.
findings.recommendation: Review server banners and HTTP headers for information disclosure; suppress unnecessary version details. Enumerate HTTP service content and configuration to verify no default pages, directory listings, or sensitive information exposure. Confirm Apache 2.4.7 patch level and apply current security updates. Review TLS/HTTPS configuration if available.

findings.title: nping-echo Service Exposed on Port 9929 with Unknown Version
findings.evidence: nmap identified a network-exposed service on port 9929 identified as nping-echo with unknown version. The service is externally accessible but does not match a specialized mapping rule.
findings.recommendation: Validate service necessity and document the business purpose. Review software version and confirm it is current. Review access restrictions and configuration. Determine whether this service should be exposed externally or restricted to internal networks.

findings.title: Unknown Service on Port 31337 (tcpwrapped)
findings.evidence: nmap identified an unknown service on port 31337 marked as tcpwrapped with unknown version. The service is externally exposed but identity and purpose are unclear.
findings.recommendation: Identify and document the purpose of the service on port 31337; verify whether it is intentional. Review software version and confirm current patch status. Review access restrictions and configuration. Implement firewall rules to restrict access if appropriate.

next_steps: Prioritize verification of SSH authentication policy - confirm password authentication is disabled and root login is restricted
Conduct patch-level assessment for SSH version 6.6.1p1 and Apache 2.4.7 against current Ubuntu security advisories
Perform web service enumeration on port 80 to identify exposed content, default pages, and sensitive information
Identify and validate business necessity for services on ports 9929 and 31337
Implement or review firewall rules and network segmentation to restrict access to non-essential services
Suppress unnecessary service version information in server banners and HTTP headers
